steps.title: Compare linear and flexible models
steps.content: Text 1 presents conventional wisdom as a linear sequence from equal hunter-gatherer bands to agriculture-driven hierarchy. Text 2 says humans have always been socially flexible, with hunter-gatherers alternating between decentralized and hierarchical structures long before agriculture.

steps.title: Reject responses Text 2 lacks
steps.content: Choice A says Mendes and Arden rank decentralized societies as more significant, which they do not. Choice C contradicts their evidence of preagricultural hierarchy. Choice D says they challenge whether hunter-gatherers were early social structures, but they challenge the staged progression. The answer is B.
